name: Campaign Planning Template
summary: A structured template for planning any marketing campaign from objective to execution to measurement.
whenToUseIt: Use before launching any campaign — whether it's a product launch, seasonal promotion, brand awareness push, or lead generation drive. Prevents the common mistake of jumping straight into tactics without a plan.
steps: Objective: What specific, measurable goal are you trying to achieve? (e.g., 500 new leads, 20% sales increase, 10,000 email signups)
Target Audience: Who specifically are you reaching? Use your personas.
Key Message: What's the single most important thing you want the audience to think, feel, or do?
Channels: Where will you reach them? (Social, email, paid ads, PR, events, partnerships)
Budget: How much will you spend? Break it down by channel.
Timeline: When does the campaign launch, run, and end? Key milestones?
Creative Brief: What assets do you need? (Copy, images, video, landing pages)
KPIs: How will you measure success? Set targets for each metric.
Post-Campaign Review: What worked? What didn't? What will you do differently next time?
example: Campaign plan for a Black Friday sale: Objective = 40% revenue increase vs. last year. Audience = existing customers + warm leads. Message = 'Our biggest sale of the year — up to 50% off everything.' Channels = email (3 emails), Instagram stories, Google Shopping ads, website banner. Budget = $3,000 (50% paid ads, 30% email platform, 20% creative). Timeline = teaser 1 week before, launch day, 3-day event, follow-up. KPIs = revenue, conversion rate, email open rate, ROAS.
